## ServeOptions interface

Options for [servePlugin()](./stripe-cli-plugin-bootstrap.serveplugin.md)<!-- -->.

**Signature:**

```typescript
export interface ServeOptions
```

## Remarks

- When [ServeOptions.networkType](./stripe-cli-plugin-bootstrap.serveoptions.networktype.md) is "tcp" and the `address` omits a port, an ephemeral port is chosen automatically. - When [ServeOptions.networkType](./stripe-cli-plugin-bootstrap.serveoptions.networktype.md) is "unix", the address is treated as a filesystem path and will be prefixed with `unix:` for gRPC if not already present.

## Example

Using multiple protocol versions:

```ts
await servePlugin({
  versionedPlugins: {
    1: new PluginV1(),
    2: new PluginV2(),
    3: new PluginV3(),
  },
  address: '127.0.0.1:0',
  pluginMetadata: {
    name: 'generate',
    version: '0.0.1',
  },
})
```

</td><td>

Address to bind. - For "tcp", use `host:port` (e.g. `127.0.0.1:0` to select an ephemeral port) - For "unix", provide the socket path (e.g. `/tmp/my.sock`<!-- -->)

</td></tr>

</td><td>

_(Optional)_ Network type for the gRPC server. Defaults to "tcp".

</td></tr>

</td><td>

</td><td>

[PluginMetadata](./stripe-cli-plugin-bootstrap.pluginmetadata.md)

</td><td>

_(Optional)_ Plugin metadata for telemetry. If provided, the bootstrap will automatically send analytics events for command lifecycle (started, completed, duration, error).

</td></tr>

[versionedPlugins](./stripe-cli-plugin-bootstrap.serveoptions.versionedplugins.md)

</td><td>

</td><td>

[VersionedPlugins](./stripe-cli-plugin-bootstrap.versionedplugins.md)

</td><td>

Map of protocol versions to plugin implementations. Allows serving multiple protocol versions simultaneously. The CLI will negotiate which version to use during the handshake.
